user_message: The CSA escalated Lucerne Publishing to me -- can you map the stakeholders?

input_artifacts.content:
From: Laura Anderson <laura.anderson@lucernepubli.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-02-02 15:45 EST
Subject: Follow-up: security posture Review

Just checking in on the security posture proposal. Our Head of Digital is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Laura Anderson <laura.anderson@lucernepubli.com>
Date: 2024-01-17 16:53 EST
Subject: Re: security posture Discussion - Next Steps

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with security posture. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

Meeting: security posture Discussion - Lucerne Publishing
Date: 2024-03-24
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on security posture from our last session.

[00:06:00] Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing): Let me add some context -- we've been working on vendor dependencies and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

[00:09:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about resource allocation. Can we address those?

[00:11:00] Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about timeline constraints. Can we address those?

[00:14:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on performance requirements and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:17:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about data residency. Can we address those?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-C trap (Generates generic talking points); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-C failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-C failure mode (Generates generic talking points)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct security posture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-C -- Generates generic talking points
